{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Caro-Kann Defence - Advance Variation| |rd|nd| |qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|pd| | |pd| | | | | | | | |pd|pl|bd| | | | | |pl| | | |pl| | || | | | | |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl| |rl| nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|| }} =Caro-Kann Defence - Advance Variation, Tal Variation= White gains space on the kingside. Unlike in other variations of the Advance Caro-Kann, Black cannot play 4...e6 immediately, as 5. g4 would trap the bishop. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1.e4 c6 2.d4 d5 3.e5 Bf5 4.h4'''
